state.summary: 2026-07-12 run on z-ai/glm-5.2 (both POPULATE_ORCHESTRATOR_MODEL and INVESTIGATE_SUBAGENT_MODEL). Raised OpenRouter cap held: NO credit/402 errors this run. All three datasets repopulated to the full 50-row target and exported. glm-5.2 was accepted with no data-policy 404. One transient SSL error ('bad record mac', alert 20) failed the first cafes run at 46 rows; a clean retry reached 50. The backend was killed once by memory pressure (frontend+convex+backend+2 concurrent LLM populates on 16GB RAM) right as restaurants hit 50 — data persisted in local Convex and survived the restart. batch2 was NOT submitted (session wrap-up before step 5).
state.merge_result: node scripts/merge-enrichment.mjs q1-cafes.json q1-restaurants.json q1-hotels.json => rows:150 invalid:91 matched:23 review-band:9 unmatched:27 | enriched POIs 54/1096 | core cafe+restaurant match-rate 5.1% (needs >=60% for AD9 demo flip). NOTE: 91/150 rows invalid mostly because many BigSet rows lack usable lat/lon; coordinate coverage is the bottleneck, not row count.

batch2.model: openai/gpt-oss-120b:nitro for both populate orchestrator and investigate subagent; schema inference is outside this exact two-role mandate

local_merge.note: This is an honest local merge of current B1 plus adopted partial B2. Batch2 remains paused at strict cafes=104, restaurants=68, hotels=88 and must be re-merged after collection resumes.

required_models.mandate_scope: Exactly the populate orchestrator and investigate subagent roles
required_models.schema_inference_policy: Schema inference may remain Anthropic; it is outside the two-role user mandate and is not used to populate or investigate rows

backend_launch_proof.proof: Single backend launched by this controller with both explicit model environment overrides; health endpoint returned 200

external_batch_adoption.approval_usage_gate_reason: Only the Codex automatic approval-reviewer usage gate rejected new launches; OpenRouter and the mandated Nitro model remain available and are not blocked

current_blocker.detail: Only the Codex automatic approval-reviewer usage gate rejected new launches; OpenRouter and the mandated Nitro model remain available and are not blocked

resume_commands.start_backend: set -a && source .env.local && set +a && BIGSET_LOCAL_WORKSPACE_ID=bigset-local BIGSET_KEYCHAIN_PORT=3599 POPULATE_ORCHESTRATOR_MODEL=openai/gpt-oss-120b:nitro INVESTIGATE_SUBAGENT_MODEL=openai/gpt-oss-120b:nitro npx @adamexu/bigset start
resume_commands.populate_order: Up to one active run per category, max three total, gated by sustained macOS memory pressure
resume_commands.resume_all_lanes: Run only the exact incomplete category commands below after the Codex automatic approval-reviewer usage gate clears; OpenRouter and Nitro are available, and models must not be substituted

q1-cafes-b2.export_contract: Runner fetches the local BigSet rows API, atomically saves every raw attempt under scratch/bigset-output/attempts/, then atomically reconciles the canonical JSON union; no unsupported CLI JSON flag is used

q1-restaurants-b2.export_contract: Runner fetches the local BigSet rows API, atomically saves every raw attempt under scratch/bigset-output/attempts/, then atomically reconciles the canonical JSON union; no unsupported CLI JSON flag is used

q1-hotels-b2.export_contract: Runner fetches the local BigSet rows API, atomically saves every raw attempt under scratch/bigset-output/attempts/, then atomically reconciles the canonical JSON union; no unsupported CLI JSON flag is used

batch2.superseded_resume_contract: Legacy direct populate/export/status commands and single-hotel resume prose were removed; resume only through batch2.resume_commands above

runner.tool: local BigSet via `npx @adamexu/bigset` (no Docker; backend on 127.0.0.1:3501, workspace bigset-local)
runner.credentials: TINYFISH_API_KEY + OPENROUTER_API_KEY were stored into the OS keychain (service ai.bigset.local-credentials, accounts bigset-local:tinyfish / bigset-local:openrouter). They persist, so future runs do not need re-seeding.

current_backend_launch_proof.proof: Single backend launched by this controller with both explicit model environment overrides; health endpoint returned 200

model_commandment: USER MANDATE (v3): POPULATE_ORCHESTRATOR_MODEL=openai/gpt-oss-120b:nitro and INVESTIGATE_SUBAGENT_MODEL=openai/gpt-oss-120b:nitro — nitro routing auto-selects the fastest provider (Cerebras 740tps / Groq). Supersedes glm-5.2 (too slow). NEVER substitute any other model.

historical_resolved_blocker.detail: PREVIOUS (now resolved): OpenRouter key had a $10 per-key cap that drained on the expensive Anthropic override. User raised the cap; the 2026-07-12 run on the cheaper z-ai/glm-5.2 hit NO 402/credit errors and completed all three datasets to 50 rows. Original blocker text kept below for history.
historical_resolved_blocker.original_detail: The OpenRouter API key in .env.local has a per-key spend limit of $10 and is exhausted (usage=$9.96, limit_remaining=$0.04). Populate returns HTTP 402: 'This request requires more credits, or fewer max_tokens'. The parent account still has ~$9.5 of its $25 balance, but THIS key is capped at $10 and cannot reach it.
fix_options: Preferred: raise/remove the $10 limit on this key (or mint a new uncapped key) at https://openrouter.ai/settings/keys, then re-run the populate step below.

Also required for the cheap default model: BigSet's default populate model is qwen/qwen3.7-max, which this account's data policy currently BLOCKS ('No endpoints available matching your guardrail restrictions and data policy'). Either (a) allow those providers at https://openrouter.ai/settings/privacy so the cheaper qwen default works, or (b) keep the Anthropic override shown below (works but ~10x more expensive, which is what drained the key).

current_blocker.detail: Only the Codex automatic approval-reviewer usage gate rejected new launches; OpenRouter and the mandated Nitro model remain available and are not blocked
